posts.cap: "I keep waiting for Spence to leave. Because I can't wrap my mind around why he would stay. Because you didn't.". Tatyana Ali and Charles S. Dutton. Come Back Dad. Streaming free on VURT. @tatyanaali  #VURT #ComeBackDad #TatyanaAli #CharlesSDutton #DramaTok

"I got straight A's. I got awards. I got accepted into one of the finest medical institutions in this nation. And my father complained the whole time about you.". Some wounds go all the way back.. Come Back Dad. Streaming free on VURT. Myvurt.com #VURT #ComeBackDad #DramaTok #FamilyDrama

clips.name: Comeback-1.mp4
clips.txt: No, you fail every day. You know, you hated me since we were little. Yes, I have. I got straight A's. I got awards. I got accepted into one of the finest medical institutions in this nation. And my father complained the whole time about you. He couldn't even acknowledge our accomplishments without saying that he was glad that they were not your failures. You were his beloved project. He reveled in your dysfunction. The tortured artiste. Well, we worked hard for his love, and he showered you with it. Oh, Daddy hated me, and you know it. He didn't hate you.

clips.name: ComeBack-2.mp4
clips.txt: What about when I paid for you to go back to school 10 years ago and you quit? What about when I paid for your first car? You were too cheap to pay for the insurance. What about when I paid for the bulk of your wedding to my best friend? Jeanette was my best friend and you ruined it.

clips.name: VURT_COMEBACKDAD_Social 02_1.mp4
clips.txt: Showing up to gigs drunk. I punched a guy in the audience, Odell. He was heckling me, man. He was clapping, O. Look, I need this, Benny. I'm clean. How long? 2 months, 4 days, and 7 and a half hours. Oh. I'm rooting for you more than anybody. But you gotta prove you can be a reliable musician, man. Yeah, we got another spot for a piano player, but I can only give it to someone who's gonna show up on time, come to rehearsal without a bottle in his hand, and not throw up during a set, brother. It happened once. It was on the bass player.

clips.name: VURT_COMEBACKDAD_Social 05_1.mp4
clips.txt: The bride's father, Mr. Othello Babineaux. [FOREIGN LANGUAGE]

clips.name: VURT_COMEBACKDAD_Social 04.mp4
clips.txt: It's been, you know, it's been a while. See, most of the time I like fix cars, and so it's been a while. Stop wasting my time.

clips.name: VURT_FavoriteSon_Social_Edit_001-v2.mp4
clips.txt: Uh, we shouldn't. Yeah, you're right. Or we could skip dinner and— I thought we were trying to wait till— Till marriage? Are you gonna let me in on when that's gonna be?

clips.name: VURT_FavoriteSon_Social_Edit_004-v2.mp4
clips.txt: I know you have responsibilities. So do I. But constantly covering for you not showing up shouldn't be one of them. So I missed your little meeting. Oh, don't do that. I will make the next one. You promised me no matter what that you would prioritize home first. I am sick of you putting the church and her before me. I didn't sign up for that. And after all these years— You know what I do. You know when I'm not here, I am taking care of business, trying to expand while keeping us and the church afloat. Yes, I know what you do. And you need to figure out a better way. Because if you don't— Rita. I need you to show up for us! Billy! I need you to be where I need you to be! I am more than your party planner and your little organizer. I am your wife, and you will respect me, and you will put your family first.

clips.name: VURT_FavoriteSon_Social_Edit_002.mp4
clips.txt: Dad. Right here. What happened tonight better not ever happen again. You hear me? You sure voted for Royce London. I wasn't. Of course you did. That's my church. You hear me? You play what I tell you to play. And you do what I tell you to do.

clips.name: VURT_FavoriteSon_Social_Edit_003.mp4
clips.txt: So, Cam, you'll update your dad on all the talking points then? Yes, ma'am. I'll let him know. The Foundation would also like for the entire family to stick around for for a group photo with the volunteers after So Gifted performs. Yes, of course. Great. I will give them a call and follow up with the caterers. Is there anything else for Sadie? Oh, no, Regina. Thank you. Oh, just update the rest of the fundraising committee for me. You got it. Thank you. Baby, you okay? You look tired. Yeah. Late night. Just working on some new songs. Oh. Workaholic, just like your daddy. He stayed overnight studying in his office again. Oh.

clips.name: VURT_FavoriteSon_Social_Edit_005.mp4
clips.txt: He's going to change the face of gospel. Mm-hmm. Pastors the church in Houston for me. Mm-hmm. Oh, I didn't realize you finalized plans, Dad. When you opening it? Oh, it'll be at least a year from now. I'm going to start with online services weekly from here, and then Blaine He's gonna move to Houston and run it all. Praise the Lord. Both of our sons walking into their destinies. Amen.

clips.txt: So you saying if I pledge, everything on the table for me, but if I don't pledge, everything off the table? Bingo. That simple. And back to the hood you go without an offer.

clips.name: KARMA_IN_HEELS_SocialEdit_002-v3.mp4
clips.txt: Back after introduction. I see you tonight. I booked everything. I'mma send you the information. Okay. I can't believe I'm about to lose my virginity to the man of my dream.

clips.name: KARMA_IN_HEELS_SocialEdit_003-v3.mp4
clips.txt: What the fuck? You, you did this to me. You did this to me. Our special night, I was just a game. You did this to me. I was just a toy on your initiation night. Charisma, what are you talking about? I hate you. Charisma!

clips.name: KARMA_IN_HEELS_SocialEdit_004-v3.mp4
clips.txt: Oh, and I'm feeling you. Feeling me? You don't even know me. You see that smile? That shit is so sexy, baby. Why are you following me? What do you want from me? I just want to get to know you.

clips.name: KARMA_IN_HEELS_SocialEdit_005-v3.mp4
clips.txt: And you! Your stupid brother and your stupid fraternity friends made a complete mockery of me! Wait— no... No, don't be so surprised Tyler! I got a surprise for you better than this old thing.

clips.name: KARMA_IN_HEELS_SocialEdit_006-v3.mp4
clips.txt: You came here to do a job. He doesn't know the new you from a can of paint. Okay? I need you to own this shit. Yeah? Okay.

clips.name: KARMA_IN_HEELS_SocialEdit_007-v3.mp4
clips.txt: Fuck outta here. You gonna feel this shit, every inch. And you better remember every fucking thing they did to you.

clips.name: KARMA_IN_HEELS_SocialEdit_008-v3.mp4
clips.txt: You're telling me that the man that raped you was standing on that podium preaching and you're catching the Holy Ghost? Quit. Go back.

clips.name: KARMA_IN_HEELS_SocialEdit_009-v3.mp4
clips.txt: I know we already getting this party started without me, sister. I would never. I wanna play. Especially with this motherfucker. Who are you talking to? Leave me alone!

clips.name: KARMA_IN_HEELS_SocialEdit_010-v3.mp4
clips.txt: Wow! Welcome to the Eta Chi Psi grad chapter sisterhood. My goodness, girl, you are gorgeous. Well, thank you so much. Yes, it's giving like melanin goddess. Not like you already. So sign your name here, put your contact information, and we're going to take you into the intake room.

We’re making history. 🌍 We’re premiering the world’s first African original vertical drama, an industry first that shifts the way stories are told and shared across the globe. We are excited to announce that “The Love Jam Network” is coming soon to myvurt.com  This isn’t just another movie premiere. We are creating pathways for creators everywhere to reach audiences on their own terms. We’re excited to premiere @BinyumaTV from writer @Ruyonga .   If  you're a filmmaker with a story to tell, VURT is built for you. Submit your film. Be live in 48 hours. Create. Submit. Engage. #VURT #IndieFilm #VerticalVideo #Filmmakers #ShortForm

clips.name: ru.mp4
clips.txt: What's up? My name is Ryonga, aka Roo. Real bad man. I'm an actor, I'm a rapper, I'm a singer-songwriter. I play Roo on The Love Network. Roo is a connector. He's what some would call a fixer or a handler. Roo knows everyone, up top, in the middle, at the bottom. Ru is an everyman's man. This man! Everyone likes him. Everyone knows him. Sometimes it's to his benefit. Sometimes it's not. You get it? He happens to be one of the best friends of Izo, Ezekiel, played by Izo Isaac. A lot of people have their names around here. Essi is Essi. Ru is Ru. Izo is Izo. Ru and Prince are Izo's friends. Ru wants to connect his people and take them up the ladder, but he feels like he wants to teach them how to to observe protocol, and Izo doesn't have time for that stuff. For him, he's just trying to jump the ladder. So it really begins with him trying to let him know, with Roo trying to let Izo know that that person is not your level. And when Izo doesn't listen, he gets in trouble, and that's where all the madness begins. That's where the Love Network jam just kicks off. Um, I'm a producer on this project. Shout out to Benjima TV, along with a few other people. I bumped into verticals a few months ago. I realized I'd been watching them and watching them, and I was like, eh, number one, what are these things? Then I found out they were verticals. And number two, I've never seen an African vertical yet. I feel like so many Africans are watching them. What's that about? And I think going down that rabbit hole led me to be in a place where we put together a platform, and we're doing the first— one of the first, if not the first— vertical movie in Africa with African content. Basically, you call it the first TikTok movie, not just a clip with multiple clips that make up a movie. Benyama TV is a platform that is going to allow creatives to expand their horizons, try out, you know, material, try out and conceptualize— more than conceptualize, manifest and materialize their ideas without waiting for a big budget. I'm a fan of the Solos, the Primes, the Mama Peshis who do these short-form movies on TikTok. And I'm like, if you lengthen them a bit, you could basically be doing verticals. So what we did is we put together a platform that is going to showcase African creativity, African verticals, African storytelling, African stories, African movies, where we figure it out and we do it on our budget, just like all these TikTokers have been doing, making incredible content. But you will also get paid from it. But that is a whole other story and a whole other discussion we will have when the time is right. But right now, be on the lookout for The Love Network. Now I'm coming to your screens, like right now. My name is Edwin Ryonga. I play Roo on The Love Network. Kaache on Binyamati.

clips.name: Felicity.mp4
clips.txt: My name is Felicity Mandela, and I play the character Denise in Love Network. This is a vertical, first of its kind, and the story is about urban crime. You know how, um, day to day there's different things that go on about our lives, but now this is giving perspective of an urban crime story from Africa and how everything is running around. And this lady, this young lady, has power, but you'll be surprised what looks like a coincidence or what looks like an honest mistake of forgetting unruffles everyone's world just to get back a small gadget. So I love the story. Everybody's rattled just to get a small gadget. Yes. Verticals are the new future, and this being the first of its kind happening here is a very good thing because society now is living their life on the phone. And so what verticals have come to do is have your entertainment on the go anywhere you are. You don't have to wait to go to, you know, the cinema or go home to watch a movie. It's now right here on the phone. So I actually appreciate that, that you don't have to overthink it. Have entertainment on the go. [MUSIC] Benyuma TV is the first of its kind here in Africa, and it's focusing on verticals, but specifically showing and telling African stories by Africans, made by Africans, to the world. So we're not having our stories being told for us, but this time around we're telling our own stories, showing you our perspective. From us. Yes, so that's what Benyuma TV is all about. It's merging different African stories told by Africans to the world. My name is Felicity Mandela. Catch me on Benyuma TV.

clips.name: Essy.mp4
clips.txt: Hello, SB360! My name is Mahoro Esther. I am an actress, I am a host, I am a content creator, but for this particular project, I am acting as Effie. So this story revolves around a phone. There is this very beautiful boss babe that loses a phone. And the guy that has a crush on her tries to get the phone back to her, but in the process, he encounters a lot of difficulties. He's abducted, he's kidnapped, he's threatened. He finds himself in a position where he's supposed to work with a thief to catch a thief. And he's also working with a group of guys that are helping him. So he eventually— I don't know if he eventually— we don't know if he eventually gets the phone to her. You just have to watch the story. You know what happens. It's a movie that is shot like this instead of this, and it's usually for smartphone consumers, you know, because people don't want to first go to their homes, turn on the TVs to watch movies. Right now you can just watch those verticals on your phone without even having to rotate it. Binyuma TV is a platform that's going to stream vertical movies, the first of its kind, I must say, in Africa. So think Netflix, but for vertical movies, okay? Vertical movies from Africa. So there's going to be a lot of genres on there. There's going to be drama, comedy, thrillers, political movies, all sorts of genres. My name is Mahoro Esther. I play Essie on The Love Network, Tachitumbi. [MUSIC] [MUSIC]

clips.name: Eezo.mp4
clips.txt: Pretty little African girl, girl, you are the queen, you are my world. My name is Isaac Haweka. I am an artist. I do sing, write, perform music. I do perform poetry and write it as well. And right here on this one, I am an actor. That's why I want to call myself an artist. On The Love Network, I play Izo, a go-getter. He's a hustler. He chases paper and he's a dreamer. He dreams quite big on, uh, on business and making it, chasing the bag, but also dreams so much in love life. So he chases love but also chases money and does everything in his power to, to get what he wants. Yes, I've bumped into verticals on social media, on Instagram, and Shorts on YouTube. Instagram, I've— most of the verticals I've landed on are from Korea, um, some are from the States, yet others, uh, it's Korea in Asia. Yeah, most of them are Asian. I've seen some American, and I've also seen some Spanish, Spanish verticals, but mostly on Instagram and Shorts of YouTube. I'm very excited because Africans hadn't embraced this, but I mean, whether I like it or not, that's where we headed. So the earlier Africa gets on it, the better for entertainment. Benyuma TV is a vertical platform. It's an online platform that is more like a hub for African content, which is in vertical, something that has not been the usual. I haven't seen any in Uganda, not any in East Africa. I haven't seen any in Africa. So this should be the very first one, vertical platform for this kind of content. My name is Isaac Haweka, AKA Izo Wekzi, and I play Izo on The Love Network. Catch it. On Benyuma TV.
